prompt: Design a curriculum to teach a base model (Llama-3.2-1B - intentionally small) to use a scratchpad-style chain-of-thought for elementary-school arithmetic word problems. Output must show step-by-step working bracketed in <scratch>...</scratch> tags before the final answer in <answer>N</answer>. Output stages with (dataset, success criterion).
reference_solution:
Curriculum: teach Llama-3.2-1B scratchpad CoT for elementary arithmetic.

Stage 1 - Basic arithmetic fluency.
  Dataset: 20k (problem, single-line-answer) for addition/subtraction within 100, then multiplication/division within 12x12. Source: programmatic generation.
  Success: 95% accuracy on 500 single-op test set.

Stage 2 - Scratchpad format introduction.
  Dataset: 15k (one-sentence-problem, <scratch>step1\nstep2</scratch><answer>N</answer>) for simple two-op problems. Source: generate by templating stage-1 with structured working-out.
  Success: 95% format-compliance (scratch + answer blocks present and well-formed) on 1k held-out; 85% answer-correctness.

Stage 3 - Word-problem variety.
  Dataset: 8k GSM8K-easy subset (problems with answer <= 100 and <= 3 ops). Reformatted with the <scratch>/<answer> template.
  Success: 70% accuracy on a 500-example word-problem eval; 90% format-compliance.

Stage 4 - Distractor-information robustness.
  Dataset: 4k word problems augmented with 1-2 sentences of irrelevant information ('Alice has 5 apples. Her cousin lives in Seattle. She gives 2 to Bob...'). Reference scratchpad explicitly ignores distractors.
  Success: accuracy maintained within 5pts of stage-3 baseline; distractor-mention-in-scratch <= 10%.

Stage 5 - Held-out eval.
  Dataset: GSM8K test subset filtered to <= 3 ops and answer <= 1000 (~400 problems).
  Success: 50% accuracy (Llama-3.2-1B base is ~25%); scratch-block present in 95% of responses.
